ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "given_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "family_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "hired_date" TEXT /* Date when the user was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date when the user left the organization (if applicable) */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the user's emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user's emergency contact */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "communication_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each preference record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the preference belongs */,
  "do_not_call" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the user prefers not to be contacted via phone */,
  "do_not_email" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the user prefers not to be contacted via email */,
  "preferred_contact_time" TEXT /* Preferred time of day for communication */,
  "last_contact_date" TEXT /* Date when the user was last contacted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communication_preferences_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department within the organization */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the department */,
  "department_head_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department head */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the department */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Budget allocated to the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_department_head_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_head_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
